name: American Bullfrog
summary: The American bullfrog, often simply known as the bullfrog in Canada and the United States, is a large true frog native to eastern North America. It typically inhabits large permanent water bodies such as swamps, ponds, and lakes. Bullfrogs can also be found in manmade habitats such as pools, koi ponds, canals, ditches and culverts. The bullfrog gets its name from the sound the male makes during the breeding season, which sounds similar to a bull bellowing. They are generalist carnivores, consuming a wide variety of prey. In turn, they are eaten by predators such as alligators, snakes, and birds.

wikipedia_sections.etymology: The bullfrog gets its name from the sound the male makes during the breeding season , which sounds similar to a bull bellowing. Taxonomy [ edit ] The specific name , catesbeiana ( feminine ) or catesbeianus (masculine), is in honor of English naturalist Mark Catesby . 3 Some authorities use the scientific name , Lithobates catesbeiana , 4 although others prefer Rana catesbeiana . 5 6 7 8 A 2016 phylogenetic study of Lithobates and Rana , using mitochondrial and nuclear sequences , recovered the American bullfrog as part of a basal clade of Lithobates , more derived only than L.

wikipedia_sections.distribution: For the Asian bullfrog, see Banded bullfrog . The specific problem is: Redundant/repetitive information. Please help improve this article if you can. Bullfrogs can also be found in manmade habitats such as pools, koi ponds , canals , ditches and culverts . The bullfrog is large and is commonly eaten throughout its range, especially in the southern United States where they are plentiful. Their use as a food source has led to bullfrogs being introduced outside of their native range. Bullfrogs have been introduced into the Western United States , South America , Western Europe , China , Japan , S
wikipedia_sections.habitat: It typically inhabits large permanent water bodies such as swamps , ponds , and lakes . 29 Cannibalism has been observed in bullfrog populations in resource-limited environments. 53 The significance of forelimb sexual dimorphism allow males to remain in amplexus with the female for longer durations increasing their chance at reproductive success in the highly competitive mating environment. American bullfrog tadpoles have also "showed a preference for habitats containing structure." 61 This may reflect a lesser number of predators in these locations.
wikipedia_sections.hunting_diet: They are generalist carnivores, consuming a wide variety of prey. In turn, they are eaten by predators such as alligators , snakes, and birds. Other than for food, bullfrogs are also used for dissection in science classes. Albino bullfrogs are sometimes kept as pets, and bullfrog tadpoles are often sold at ponds or fish stores. When the frog attacks prey, the act of opening its mouth is akin to the release of a slingshot; the elastic force stored up in both the tongue and the jaw are combined to shoot the tip of the tongue toward the prey much faster than the prey's ability to see the strike a
wikipedia_sections.behavior: The territorial males that occupy sites are usually spaced some 3 to 6 m (9.8 to 19.7 ft) apart and call loudly. 45 46 At least three different types of calls have been noted in male bullfrogs under different circumstances. These male and female behaviors cause male-male competition to be high within the bullfrog population and sexual selection for the females to be an intense process. 43 Kentwood Wells postulated leks , territorial polygyny , and harems are the most likely classifications for the bullfrog mating system.
wikipedia_sections.reproduction: In addition, the unintended escape of frogs from breeding establishments or scientific research facilities, captive escapees or released pets are also a possibility. 42 Breeding season [ edit ] The bullfrog breeding season typically lasts two to three months. 43 44 A study of bullfrogs in Michigan showed the males arriving at the breeding site in late May or early June, and remaining in the area into July. 47 48 The males continuously engage in sexual activity throughout the long breeding season.
wikipedia_sections.threats: 19 It is very common on the West Coast , especially in California , where it is believed to pose a threat to the California red-legged frog , and is considered to be a factor in the decline of that vulnerable species . wikipedia_sections.conservation: ( March 2026 ) ( Learn how and when to remove this message ) American bullfrog Adult male Least Concern ( 3.1 ) 1 Secure ( NatureServe ) 2 Animalia Chordata Amphibia Order: Anura Family: Ranidae Genus: Lithobates Species: L. 40 Self-sustaining populations of American bullfrogs became established in the United Kingdom around 1999, where their introduction was likely due to accidental escapes and deliberate releases from captivity. 41 The American bullfrog has been known to spread the amphibian pathogen Batrachochytrium dendrobatidis among populations that it has been introduced to.
